research_question: How does proximity to hazardous waste facilities influence spatial patterns of asthma prevalence across census tracts of North Carolina, and how do these relationships vary spatially by socioeconomic and demographic context?
research_intent: To investigate how proximity to hazardous waste facilities affects spatial patterns of asthma prevalence across census tracts in North Carolina, with a focus on variations influenced by socioeconomic and demographic contexts.

objectives.title: Assess whether asthma prevalence is spatially clustered across North Carolina census tracts and whether hazardous-waste proximity aligns with high-asthma clusters
objectives.hypothesis: Census tracts closer to hazardous waste sites (lower nearest-site distance and/or higher site counts within 5 km) exhibit higher asthma prevalence and are overrepresented in statistically significant high-asthma clusters.

data_requirements.description: Census tract polygon boundaries and tract identifiers (GEOID) for North Carolina.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Tract geometries alone do not contain asthma prevalence or hazardous-waste proximity; proximity metrics must be derived by combining tract polygons with hazardous waste point locations, and asthma prevalence must be joined by GEOID.

what_each_contributes.NC_tract: tract polygons and GEOID for spatial weights and mapping
what_each_contributes.NC_HW_sites: hazardous waste point locations (geometry) for distance and buffer counts
what_each_contributes.NC_PLACES: tract-level asthma prevalence (Asthma_prev) to analyze spatial patterns

data_preprocessing.method: Derive tract centroids from NC_tract geometry; compute proximity metrics to NC_HW_sites; left-join NC_PLACES to tracts by GEOID.

steps.description: Reproject NC_tract and NC_HW_sites to a common projected CRS suitable for distance (meters), preserving GEOID in tracts and SITE_NAME in sites.
steps.expected_output: A GeoPackage containing projected tract polygons and projected hazardous-waste points in the same CRS for valid distance calculations.

steps.description: Compute tract centroids from NC_tract geometry and keep GEOID as the unique identifier for each centroid feature.
steps.expected_output: A GeoPackage of tract centroid points keyed by GEOID.

steps.description: Calculate for each GEOID the Euclidean distance (meters) from tract centroid to the nearest NC_HW_sites point and also count NC_HW_sites points within a 5000-meter buffer of each centroid.
steps.expected_output: A CSV keyed by GEOID with proximity metrics (e.g., nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km).

steps.description: Left-join NC_PLACES to tract_proximity_metrics.csv on GEOID to create a table containing GEOID, Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, and hw_count_5km.
steps.expected_output: A tract-keyed table of asthma prevalence and hazardous-waste proximity metrics.

steps.description: Compute Global Moran’s I for Asthma_prev using tract contiguity weights (queen) after joining Asthma_prev to NC_tract by GEOID, and report permutation-based p-values.
steps.expected_output: A JSON report of Moran’s I statistic, expected value, z-score, and permutation p-value for Asthma_prev.

steps.description: Compute Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot statistics for Asthma_prev using tract contiguity weights (queen) after joining Asthma_prev to NC_tract by GEOID, and store Gi* z-scores and significance classes per tract.
steps.expected_output: A tract GeoPackage with Gi* z-scores and hotspot/coldspot classification for Asthma_prev.

steps.description: Compare nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km between statistically significant asthma hotspots (Gi* high-high at p<0.05) and non-hotspot tracts using Mann–Whitney U tests.
steps.expected_output: A JSON summary of group medians and Mann–Whitney U test statistics and p-values for proximity differences between hotspot and non-hotspot tracts.

objectives.title: Determine how the proximity–asthma relationship varies by tract socioeconomic and demographic context and whether the relationship is spatially non-stationary
objectives.hypothesis: The association between hazardous-waste proximity and asthma prevalence is stronger in tracts with higher ratio_poverty, higher ratio_uninsured, and higher ratio_black, and the proximity coefficient varies spatially across North Carolina.

data_requirements.description: Tract-level socioeconomic and demographic covariates (ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, etc.) keyed by GEOID.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Modeling effect modification and spatial non-stationarity requires combining asthma and proximity metrics with socioeconomic/demographic covariates in a single GEOID-keyed tract dataset; no single source contains all required variables.

what_each_contributes.NC_ACS_data: context variables (ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, race/ethnicity and sex ratios)
what_each_contributes.nc_tract_centroids.gpkg: centroid coordinates needed for geographically weighted regression

data_preprocessing.method: Left-join NC_ACS_data to asthma_proximity.csv by GEOID; attach centroid coordinates from nc_tract_centroids.gpkg by GEOID.

steps.description: Left-join NC_ACS_data to asthma_proximity.csv on GEOID to create a modeling table containing Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, hw_count_5km, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, and other available ratios.
steps.expected_output: A GEOID-keyed tract table containing outcome, exposure/proximity, and socioeconomic/demographic covariates.

steps.description: Filter to complete cases for Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic, and retain GEOID for later spatial joins.
steps.expected_output: A cleaned modeling table with consistent sample size across all variables used in models.

steps.description: Create derived features including ln_nearest_hw_dist_m = ln(nearest_hw_dist_m), z-scored covariates for ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, and interaction terms ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_poverty and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_black.
steps.expected_output: A modeling-ready table with transformed proximity measures, standardized covariates, and specified interaction terms.

steps.description: Fit an OLS model with Asthma_prev as the dependent variable and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic, plus interaction terms ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_poverty and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m:ratio_black, and report heteroskedasticity-robust (HC) standard errors.
steps.expected_output: A JSON model summary with coefficients, robust standard errors, p-values, and model fit metrics.

steps.description: Join centroid coordinates (x, y) from nc_tract_centroids.gpkg to tract_modeling_features.csv by GEOID for use in geographically weighted regression.
steps.expected_output: A CSV containing all modeling features plus centroid x/y coordinates.

steps.description: Fit a geographically weighted regression (adaptive bandwidth selected by AICc; bisquare kernel) with Asthma_prev as the dependent variable and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic, and output local coefficients and local R2 at each tract centroid.
steps.expected_output: A GeoPackage of tract centroids with local coefficient surfaces (including the local ln_nearest_hw_dist_m coefficient) and local R2 for diagnosing spatial non-stationarity.
